by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BsYXVyYV8yN2JiYWZfMSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo0fQ==

Midjourney/Every illustration.

AI has entered its allocation era: Companies are starting to ask who gets access to the most powerful models, what those models should be used for, and when the cost is worth it. Today, head of tech consulting Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BtaWtlXzIxMTQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= argues that token budgets may start to look like trading portfolios, with the biggest compute budgets going to the people who can prove the biggest returns. Elsewhere, head of operations Arielle Shipper stress-tests her own AI habits, head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0B0ZWRlc2NhdSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== shares how he writes with Spiral https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3dyaXRld2l0aHNwaXJhbC5jb20vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZXZlcnl3ZWJzaXRlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9 and Codex https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2NvZGV4LW1vdmVzLWJleW9uZC1jb2RpbmciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0=, and senior designer Daniel Rodrigues https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5pZWxfNWZiZDIxXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= tracks the resurgence of trad design on social media.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2FjY291bnQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------




SIGNAL

ROI COMES FOR TOKENS

Just a few months ago, heavily subsidized AI plans and a manic push by corporations to get their employees familiar with the technology led to aggressive tokenmaxxing, or measuring AI adoption by how many tokens employees use.

That era appears to be over https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5ueXRpbWVzLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA2LzE4L3RlY2hub2xvZ3kvYWktdG9rZW4tbWluaW1pemluZy5odG1sIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ==. Shifting pricing models from the frontier labs, the transition from chatbots to long-running agents, and the emergence of powerful, incredibly costly https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NoYWluLW9mLXRob3VnaHQvdGhlLW1vcmFsLW9mLWZhYmxlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ== new models have converged to create massive AI enterprise bills, often without tangible results. Uber, Meta, Amazon, and Walmart have all moved to place caps https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5mdC5jb20vY29udGVudC8xZDM3Y2MwOC1lMGFhLTQ1YTQtYTQ1ZC00YWQyODI1MjkzMTQ_ on employee AI use.

These companies will still spend big on AI. But they’re beginning to think more

strategically—or restrictively— about how to get capable, token-hungry models and workflows in the hands of people who can maximize the return on investment (ROI).

One solution is to give engineers a set percentage of their salary to spend on tokens per month. Head of consulting Mike Taylor thinks the financial industry offers a possible model: Capable engineers will receive multiples of their salary to manage on token spend just as financial traders manage portfolios many times the size of their annual compensation. Getting the most out of current models already requires real capital—recently, Cora https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2NvcmEuY29tcHV0ZXIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZXZlcnl3ZWJzaXRlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE0fQ== general manager Kieran Klaassen https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVG9rZW4gVGlnaHRlbmluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BraWVyYW5fMTM1NSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNX0= spent $2,000 in Cursor credits in the span of a couple of days, a number that will look quaint as the models continue to improve.

newsletters.content:
I had a quarter of OKRs and no clue whether I was hitting them. Enter Codex.  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

Working Overtime

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vd29ya2luZy1vdmVydGltZSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxfQ==


I ASKED AN AI TO AUDIT MY OWN CAREER https://every.


I HAD A QUARTER OF OKRS AND NO CLUE WHETHER I WAS HITTING THEM. ENTER CODEX. https://every.

by Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo0fQ==

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vYWNjb3VudCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ== to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



I was deep in an AI-induced fugue when I remembered my OKRs.

I had published essays and guides, built agent skills for our daily newsletter, produced six hands-on reviews of new AI models https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHA6Ly9ldmVyeS50by92aWJlLWNoZWNrIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9, and accumulated enough Codex projects to make my desktop mildly alarming. But I had no idea if any of it added up to the four quarterly goals that I had promised to do by the end of June.

My brain playing a drumbeat of dread https://every., I opened my career coach project in Codex https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZ3VpZGVzL2NvZGV4LWZvci1rbm93bGVkZ2Utd29yayIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo4fQ== and typed: “Can we check in on my OKRs? Go into career coach mode.”

And in 10 minutes, I had a comprehensive, objective opinion. Yes, I had done my OKRs. Relief flooded me.

AI has finally allowed us to do a proper job of what management thinker Peter Drucker called “feedback analysis” https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vaGJyLm9yZy8yMDA1LzAxL21hbmFnaW5nLW9uZXNlbGYiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=: Write down what you expect from a major decision and compare the result months later. While old https://every. versions https://every. of career coach tools I’ve built only knew what I chose to tell them, Codex can go looking for receipts across my desktop, Slack, Google Drive, and the web. I can assemble an accurate picture of how I performed—one that’s not colored by flattery or catastrophe—and compare it to what I said I would do.

I went looking for proof that I had done my job. I came back with a much clearer idea of what my job was.


A COACH WITH A MEMORY

Before I explain how Codex saved my OKR anxieties, let me explain how I set up the career coach that allowed me to objectively track my performance. A few weeks before my OKR panic, I reorganized my desktop so both I and an agent could understand it with files and folders.

https://every. career coach folder of my desktop, complete with AGENTS.md for the agent to read first, along with folders for evidence, plans, systems, and references the agent might use in the course of our interactions. (All images courtesy of Katie Parrott.)




IN PARTNERSHIP WITH: DELETEME

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSSBBc2tlZCBhbiBBSSB0byBBdWRpdCBNeSBPd24gQ2FyZWVyIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMxNCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vam9pbmRlbGV0ZW1lLmNvbS9ldmVyeSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMywiYWR2ZXJ0aXNlbWVudF9pZCI6MTE4OH0=

As builders and followers of what comes next in technology, we know how fast the digital landscape is evolving. AI and data-driven tools are advancing rapidly, but so are the methods data brokers use to legally collect and sell your Personally Identifiable Information (PII)—like your name, address, phone number, and relatives’ names—to anyone with a credit card. This exposure leaves you vulnerable to serious risks like phishing attempts, scams, and doxing. As a community focused on the future of software and technology, taking control of your online footprint is an essential step.

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1a606641-81c4-4928-93d2-af1223d25258/65a1d52837b6a1c4fad062f0_WhatsApp_20Image_202024-01-08_20at_2010.33.34_20AM__1_.png?t=1782139923)
Caption: Courtesy: Sakana AI

**1/ A Japanese startup just challenged Anthropic's best AI models:** Sakana AI launched [Fugu Ultra](https://x.com/SakanaAILabs/status/2068861630327443966?s=20), a multi-model orchestration system that dynamically coordinates specialized AI models behind a single API. Benchmark results suggest it performs on par with Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos across coding, reasoning, science, and agent tasks.

**2/ Z.ai is challenging the economics of frontier AI:** The company released [GLM 5.2](https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.2), an open-weights model designed for long-horizon coding, research, and agent workflows with support for up to 1 million tokens of context. Early evaluations place it alongside GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Opus 4.8 while costing significantly less to run and customize. You can access it from [Z.ai](https://Z.ai)[ chat app](https://chat.z.ai/) or [Z.ai](https://Z.ai)[ API](https://docs.z.ai/).

**3/ Anthropic's most popular model could be getting its biggest upgrade yet:** [Leaks suggest](https://x.com/i/trending/2068716608168587450) Claude Sonnet 5 may launch as early as next week with a 1 million-token context window, improved coding capabilities, stronger vision performance, and better handling of complex workflows.

**4/ Tesla wants to sell AI infrastructure, not just use it:** The company has filed a trademark application for "[Megapod](https://electrek.co/2026/06/21/tesla-megapod-ai-data-center-hardware)," a modular AI data center platform that bundles compute, networking, power, and cooling into a single system. If commercialized, it could position Tesla as a competitor to Nvidia in the rapidly growing AI infrastructure market.

**5/ Hyundai is betting big on robots:** Hyundai is reportedly [acquiring](https://x.com/lukas_m_ziegler/status/2068728984297234786?s=20) SoftBank's remaining stake in the robotics company, giving it full ownership of Boston Dynamics. The move could accelerate the deployment of robots like Atlas and Spot across Hyundai's global manufacturing and logistics operations.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

On June 12, Anthropic disabled Fable 5 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2ZhYmxlLWRpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9—the most capable coding model Every had worked with—after a U.S. government ban forced the company to shut it off for everyone. There was no warning or migration window. The models we rely on, it turns out, aren’t ours to keep: A company—or a government—can switch one off overnight.

That instability is the backdrop to our week. Senior applied AI engineer Nityesh Agarwal https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BuaXR5ZXNoIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9 spent weeks building around Claude’s limitations, only to watch Anthropic’s new dynamic workflows https://every. solve the problem overnight—the cost, he says, of working at the frontier. Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BsYXVyYV8yN2JiYWZfMSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo4fQ== and Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BrYXRpZS5wYXJyb3R0MTIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= report on loops https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2xvb3BzLWZvci1ub24tY29kZXJzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== (where you give an agent a goal and it works in rounds, reviewing each result and re-prompting itself until the work is good enough) moving fast from engineering into non-technical work. Head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0B0ZWRlc2NhdSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0= built an overnight NBA simulator and reworked Every’s subscription flow the same way; Katie’s half of the piece is a playbook for what to do when a frontier model disappears. Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BtaWtlXzIxMTQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTJ9 tried something stranger, building an AI persona https://every. of GitHub COO Kyle Daigle so the real conversation could focus on what no public record could tell him—which you can hear on this week’s AI & I https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQnVpbHQgb24gTW92aW5nIEdyb3VuZCIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMTAsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3lvdXR1LmJlL09DRVZxeThrbDdRIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE0fQ==. Caption: 



----------
==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy friday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* Midjourney's strangest bet yet

* OpenAI's next model may arrive sooner than expected

* Anthropic expects its most powerful models to be back online

* OpenAI is quietly becoming a healthcare company

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **REMIO**

## [The #1 Agent with all your context](https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/742d0790-5b27-45df-b49e-f9ca5634ba4f/remioads__1_.png?t=1781880591)
Follow image link: (https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Remio

No more copy-pasting. No more starting from scratch.

Just tell remio what you need — it already knows the rest. Write docs, generate reports, draft emails, create slides, all powered by everything you've ever worked on.

Your context. Your agent. Always ready.

[Try it here](https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **PEAK OF THE DAY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6ab5975c-25a0-4cbd-920a-b769fa473566/l271_35631781840967__1_.png?t=1781878734)
Follow image link: (https://x.com/midjourney/status/2067421950314688759?s=20)
Caption: Courtesy: Midjourney

## Midjourney's strangest bet yet

Midjourney just announced something nobody had on their 2026 bingo card: [a full-body ultrasound scanner](https://www.midjourney.com/medical/blogpost).

The scanner lowers people into water and surrounds them with ultrasound sensors before scanning the entire body in about a minute. Founder David Holz says it could approach MRI-level detail while being significantly faster and easier to deploy.

_But the hardware is only part of the story._

Midjourney also plans to open dedicated "[Midjourney Spa](https://www.midjourney.com/medical)" locations in San Francisco, combining the scanners with saunas, cold plunges, gyms, and hot tubs. Holz says the company hopes to build 50,000 scanners and eventually use the technology to help perform thousands of medical diagnoses.

For a company that built its reputation [generating AI artwork](https://www.midjourney.com/explore?tab=top), the move feels completely out of left field.

_Until you look around._

OpenAI is [creeping into healthcare](https://openai.com/index/improving-health-intelligence-in-chatgpt). Amazon built its own chips. Tesla went from software to robots. The biggest AI companies aren't staying in their lanes anymore.

Which makes Midjourney's scanner less surprising.

The real surprise is how quickly AI companies are turning into something else.

([See the announcement video here](https://x.com/midjourney/status/2067421950314688759?s=20))


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/776b5ffa-e05d-4ce6-b296-6622890969d0/HLG5xEebMAAc06M__1___1_.png?t=1781878602)
Caption: Courtesy: TestingCatalog

**1/ OpenAI's next model may arrive sooner than expected: **[Reports suggest](https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-prepares-gpt-5-6-models-for-the-upcoming-release/) GPT-5.6 could launch as early as next week, bringing improvements in coding, reasoning, agent workflows, and potentially a 1.5 million token context window. OpenAI is also rumored to be lowering prices again.

**2/ Anthropic expects its most powerful models back online within days: **Anthropic says access to Mythos and Fable 5 should [return soon](https://www.koreajoongangdaily.com/business/anthropic-confident-of-reenabling-mythos-fable-5-access-in-coming-days-executive/12727522) after temporary restrictions tied to security concerns forced the company to limit availability worldwide. The models remain available through a controlled-access program used by major technology companies.

**3/ The coding model race keeps accelerating: **Z.ai released [GLM-5.2](https://x.com/Zai_org/status/2066938937344495629?s=20), a model built specifically for large-scale software projects, debugging, research, and coding agents. With a [1 million-token](https://z.ai/blog/glm-5.2) context window, the model is designed to work across entire repositories rather than individual files.

**4/ OpenAI is quietly becoming a healthcare company: **OpenAI says more than [230 million people](https://openai.com/index/improving-health-intelligence-in-chatgpt) ask ChatGPT health-related questions every week. Researchers working with OpenAI also helped identify 18 previously unsolved rare-disease diagnoses that were later confirmed by physicians.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Goal Mode in Kimi Work](https://x.com/Kimi_Moonshot/status/2067574786965061677?s=20) > A persistent execution mode that keeps your desktop agent working around the clock until a task is completed, designed for long-running objectives, complex workflows, and multi-step projects

* [Perplexity Brain](https://x.com/perplexity_ai/status/2067642139014742348) > Now integrated into Perplexity Computer, Brain acts as a continuously learning memory layer that connects every task, interaction, and insight into a unified context graph

* [Ponder](https://x.com/timwangyc/status/2054207569523024251?s=20&utm_source=www.theaivalley.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=google-wants-to-replace-your-mouse) > The agentic video editor

* [Today](https://x.com/anvisha/status/2053914822736003303?s=20&utm_source=www.theaivalley.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=google-wants-to-replace-your-mouse) > Make any PDF beautiful

* [Google Workspace Vids](https://x.com/GoogleWorkspace/status/2067249947993366654) > Instantly transforms presentations into avatar-led videos, complete with AI-generated voiceovers available in 24 languages

* [FlowMarket](https://flowmarket.social/?utm_source=www.theaivalley.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=openai-wants-ai-to-talk-like-a-human) > Orchestrates a B2B network where autonomous agents use real-time algorithmic matching to automate discovery and engagement

* [HappyOyster 1.0](https://x.com/HappyOysterAI/status/2067172393202983023) > An open-ended world model that enables real-time world generation, exploration, and interactive simulation
